question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .share() operator's reference counting behavior so that when the last subscriber cancels, the upstream Redis subscription is torn down, and a new subscriber immediately after receives messages starting from the next Redis publish rather than from where the previous stream left off, creating a gap in the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topicId that maps to the same Redis channel as another topic due to integer overflow in filter.getTopicId().getId() when formatted with topic.%d, causing messages from two distinct topics to be merged into one stream and presenting a cross-contaminated transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the subscribe() Flux has no deduplication step, so that if the Redis pub/sub channel delivers the same TopicMessage multiple times due to at-least-once delivery semantics, the subscriber receives duplicate entries with identical sequenceNumbers, making the transaction history appear to contain repeated events?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retryWhen() backoff in subscribe() to cause a window of message loss between the error and the retry, and then observe that the safety check in TopicMessageServiceImpl fills the gap with DB-retrieved messages that may have different ordering than the live Redis stream, causing a splice point where history appears reorganized?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of topicId validation within the subscribe() pipeline itself (validation only occurs at the filter level) so that a TopicMessage deserialized from Redis with a different topicId than the subscribed channel passes through and is delivered to the subscriber, injecting a foreign topic's transaction into the history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared nature of the topicMessages map so that by subscribing to a topic with a specific filter, they influence the shared Flux that other users with different filters (e.g., different startTime) also receive, causing those users to get messages that do not match their requested time window and observe a reorganized history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the subscribe() Flux does not enforce that sequenceNumber values are contiguous, so that if Redis drops a message (fire-and-forget pub/sub), the subscriber receives a stream with a gap in sequenceNumbers without any error signal, causing them to silently observe an incomplete transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doOnError() logging-only handler in subscribe() which does not terminate the stream on error before retry, causing a brief period where both the errored and retried subscriptions are active simultaneously and the subscriber receives duplicate messages from both, reorganizing the apparent transaction sequence?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Schedulers.boundedElastic() thread pool used in publishOn() to cause thread starvation by holding many long-lived subscriptions, causing message delivery to other subscribers to be delayed and reordered relative to their actual consensusTimestamp order?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getSharedListener() ignores the filter's limit and endTime when looking up or creating the shared Flux, so that a subscriber with a limit=1 filter shares the same Flux as an unlimited subscriber, and the limit enforcement happens downstream, potentially causing the limited subscriber to miss messages if the shared Flux is already past their desired range?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum message size check in the messageSerializer deserialization path to send an extremely large serialized TopicMessage to the Redis channel, causing the deserializer to allocate excessive memory and potentially corrupt the message stream for all co-subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the subscribe() pipeline maps Message::getMessage without validating the message's topicId field against the subscribed channel name, so that a message published to topic.5 but containing a TopicMessage with topicId=7 is delivered to subscribers of topic.5, injecting a foreign transaction into their history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: unsubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that unsubscribe() only removes the topic from topicMessages but does not explicitly close the ReactiveRedisMessageListenerContainer subscription, causing a zombie subscription that continues to consume Redis messages but delivers them to no one, while a new subscription created by computeIfAbsent() starts fresh and misses messages published during the zombie period?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, interval).maxBackoff(interval.multipliedBy(4L))) to cause the retry to re-subscribe to the Redis channel at a point where the channel has already received new messages, causing the subscriber to miss messages published during the backoff window and observe a history with gaps?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ConcurrentHashMap topicMessages by subscribing to thousands of distinct topicIds, causing the map to grow without bound and eventually causing memory pressure that leads to GC pauses, during which message delivery is delayed and the apparent ordering of transactions across topics is reorganized?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .share() operator's behavior where if all subscribers cancel simultaneously, the upstream is cancelled and the entry is removed from topicMessages, and then immediately re-subscribe to get a fresh Flux that starts from the next Redis message, effectively skipping any messages published between the cancel and re-subscribe, creating a gap in the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the subscribe() Flux has no watermark or checkpoint mechanism, so that after a retry the stream resumes from the next Redis message rather than from the last successfully processed sequenceNumber, causing the subscriber to miss messages published during the error/retry window?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a monotonic sequenceNumber check in the subscribe() pipeline to receive a TopicMessage with sequenceNumber=1 after already having received sequenceNumber=100, causing the subscriber to observe a history that appears to roll back to an earlier state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the container Mono is .cache()d and shared across all topic subscriptions, so that if the container encounters an error that is not retried (e.g., a fatal connection error), all topic subscriptions fail simultaneously and all subscribers lose their position in the message stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the shared Flux in topicMessages is keyed only by topic name (topic.%d) and not by any subscriber-specific filter parameters, so that two subscribers with different startTime values share the same Flux and the one with the later startTime receives messages that predate their requested window, observing a history that includes transactions before their intended start?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the subscribe() Flux does not validate the chunkNum and chunkTotal fields of received TopicMessage objects, so that a message with chunkNum=2 arrives before chunkNum=1, causing the subscriber to observe chunked messages in the wrong order and reconstruct a reorganized transaction payload?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the subscribe() Flux does not validate the validStartTimestamp field of received TopicMessage objects, so that a message with a validStartTimestamp inconsistent with its consensusTimestamp passes through unchallenged, causing the subscriber to observe a transaction that appears to have been submitted at an impossible time?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the subscribe() Flux does not enforce that the payerAccountId field of received TopicMessage objects is non-null or valid, so that a message with a null or spoofed payerAccountId passes through and is delivered to the subscriber, causing the transaction history to contain entries with incorrect attribution?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the subscribe() Flux does not enforce that the runningHashVersion field of received TopicMessage objects is within a valid range, so that a message with an invalid runningHashVersion passes through and causes downstream consumers to use the wrong hash algorithm when verifying the running hash chain, breaking the integrity of the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the subscribe() Flux does not enforce that the message field of received TopicMessage objects is within the 6KiB size limit, so that an oversized message passes through and causes downstream consumers to process a transaction that violates the protocol's size constraints, potentially corrupting the history?
